regime_justification: BOJ hawkish hold with 3 dissents, USDJPY at 159.82 near 160 watch line, MOF verbal intervention active, 3-member push for 1.0% rate signals policy tightening ahead, yet carry spread remains 187 bps and vol subdued.

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY 159.82 is 18 pips from MOF 160 watch line, Katayama verbal warnings escalated, 3 BOJ board members dissented for immediate hike to 1.0%, JGB yields near 1997 highs at 2.47%, and oil >$106/bbl drives import inflation—classic pre-intervention setup.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: USDJPY 18 pips from 160, BOJ 3-dissent hawk signal, MOF on alert; spread 187 bps still wide but pace +0.12% daily raises intervention risk.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Next BOJ meeting ~6 weeks out; 3-dissent vote implies June hike odds rising; USDJPY grinding toward 160 threshold with oil at $107 sustaining yen pressure.

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: Applied unwind prior; data ambiguous—spread wide but MOF verbal escalation + BOJ hawks suggest fragile equilibrium; FOMC today could shift USD bid; oil >$100 sustains imported inflation risk.

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: If FOMC dovish or oil retreats, pressure eases; BOJ June decision anchors next catalyst; 4-week window allows drift without forced unwind unless 160 breached with pace spike.

bottom_line: Stage 2 (Pre-Intervention): USDJPY 159.82 sits 18 pips below the MOF's 160 watch line after BOJ delivered a hawkish hold with 3 dissents for immediate hike to 1.0%. Carry spread remains 187 bps but pace is accelerating (+0.12% daily), oil at $107/bbl stokes imported inflation (¥17,090/bbl), and Katayama's verbal warnings are escalating. VIX 18.6 and MOVE 68.7 signal complacency, yet JGB 10Y at 2.47% (near 1997 highs) and Nikkei -1.27% reflect mounting stress. Grades C/C/C/D: 1-2 week risk elevated by proximity to 160 and hawkish BOJ pivot; 3-week applies unwind prior to ambiguous setup; 4-week downgraded if FOMC or oil relieve pressure. Trigger map: MOF intervention cocked, BOJ hike surprise armed, JGB breakout armed. Watch FOMC today, USDJPY pace through 160, and June BOJ meeting rhetoric.

stack.note: 18 pips below MOF 160 watch line; grinding higher post-BOJ hold
US 10Y 4.35%, JGB 10Y 2.47% (near 1997 high); spread compressed by JGB spike
Subdued pre-FOMC; carry-to-vol proxy ~24.3 (spread/vol = 187/7.7), elevated but vol not pricing stress
Near 29-year high; BOJ hold + 3 dissents pushed yields higher, signaling hawkish shift
One-month high pre-FOMC; oil-driven inflation concerns keeping yields elevated
Hormuz closure, UAE OPEC exit; ¥/bbl ≈17,090 (106.88 × 159.82), major imported inflation driver
Below 80 threshold; rates-vol complacent despite geopolitical oil shock
Below 20; equity vol rising but still subdued relative to energy disruption scale
Retreated from record highs post-BOJ; SoftBank -10%, tech selloff on AI earnings miss
Consolidating below $80k; risk-on bid fading, correlation to DXY rising as Japan carry unwind chatter builds

trigger_map.note: USDJPY 159.82, 18 pips from 160; Katayama verbal warnings escalated, authorities 'ready to respond around the clock'; last intervention ~¥2.8tn Sept 2022; rate-check rumors likely if pace spikes through 160.

3 of 9 board members (Nakagawa, Takata, Tamura) dissented for immediate hike to 1.0%; Ueda cited 'Middle East uncertainty' but rhetoric shifting hawkish; next meeting ~June, but inter-meeting move possible if yen collapses or inflation accelerates.

trigger_map.trigger: JGB 10Y break above prior cycle high
trigger_map.note: JGB 10Y at 2.47%, just below 2.496% April 13 high (highest since 1997); break above 2.50% would signal full normalization underway, compressing carry further and raising unwind odds.
MOVE 68.7, well below 100 threshold; rates-vol remains compressed despite oil shock; spike would indicate bond market repricing Fed/BOJ paths, leading equity vol.

trigger_map.trigger: Energy / Hormuz shock pushing ¥/bbl > prior peak
trigger_map.note: WTI $107, ¥/bbl ≈17,090; Hormuz closed ~20% global oil, IEA calls it 'largest supply shock on record'; if WTI >$120, ¥/bbl >19,000 would force BOJ hand on inflation, accelerating normalization and carry unwind.

narrative.p1: BOJ held at 0.75% Tuesday but delivered a hawkish hold: 3 of 9 board members dissented for an immediate hike to 1.0%, the largest hawkish bloc since normalization began. Ueda cited 'Middle East uncertainty' yet raised core CPI forecast to 2.8% (from 1.9%) and cut growth to 0.5%, acknowledging stagflationary pressure from the Iran war and Hormuz closure. USDJPY drifted to 159.82 (+0.12% daily), now 18 pips from the MOF's 160 watch line. Finance Minister Katayama escalated verbal warnings, stating authorities are 'ready to respond around the clock' to 'excessive moves' and referencing the Japan-US joint statement as justification for 'bold action.' Oil spiked +6.96% to $107/bbl on reports Trump is preparing to extend the Iran blockade and the UAE's exit from OPEC; Japan's ¥/bbl import cost hit ≈17,090, the highest since the conflict began. Nikkei fell -1.27%, led by SoftBank (-10%) on AI sector weakness. VIX rose +4.6% to 18.6, but MOVE ticked only +2.2% to 68.7—cross-asset vol remains eerily subdued. Stage shifted from 1 (Pressure Building) to 2 (Pre-Intervention) on proximity to 160, escalated MOF rhetoric, and BOJ dissent signaling June hike in play.
narrative.p2: Quantitatively: US-JP 10Y spread compressed -6 bps to 187 bps (US 10Y 4.35%, JGB 10Y 2.47%) as JGBs sold off to near-1997 highs; carry remains wide but the rate of change is accelerating. USD/JPY 1M implied vol ≈7.7 is subdued, yielding a carry-to-vol proxy of ~24.3 (187/7.7)—historically elevated, suggesting carry still attractive but fragile. USDJPY 5-day pace +0.6% is moderate, yet daily +0.12% pace is what MOF watches; sustained moves at this clip through 160 historically trigger intervention. Distance from 60d mean: USDJPY 159.82 vs. ~155 mean = +3.1% stretched, not extreme but nearing intervention zone. Stage 2 (Pre-Intervention) fits: we're at the watch line, rate-checks rumored (Katayama's 'bold action' language), vol bid emerging (VIX up, though MOVE lagging), and the BOJ pivot (3 dissents) signals the policy reaction function is shifting. 1-week grade C: proximity to 160 + pace + MOF on alert = elevated intervention risk, though no trigger yet. 2-week grade C: June BOJ meeting ~6 weeks out, but 3-dissent vote implies inter-meeting hike odds rising if yen breaks sharply; oil >$100 sustains pressure. 3-week grade C with unwind prior applied: data genuinely ambiguous—spread still wide (187 bps), vol low (7.7), equities sold off but not cascading—yet MOF verbal escalation, BOJ hawks, and oil shock create a fragile setup where a single catalyst (160 break, pace spike, or Ueda hawkish shift) could flip to Stage 3. Without the prior, would grade D (wide spread, low vol, no forced unwind underway). 4-week grade D: if FOMC today is dovish or oil retreats, pressure eases; BOJ June decision is the next major catalyst, and 4-week window allows for drift without forced unwind unless 160 is breached with sustained pace.
narrative.p3: BTC consolidating at $76,380 (-0.63% 1d), down -3.7% over 7 days, holding below the $80k resistance it tested earlier this month. Institutional BTC ETF flows mixed: Strategy kept buying, but US spot ETFs saw -$273M outflows last week, signaling waning risk appetite. XRP at $1.38 (-0.5% 1d) mirrors the drift, with spot XRP ETFs posting +$83.9M inflows in April (best since January) yet price unable to break $1.45 resistance—60% of supply held at $1.44 avg cost basis creates a sell wall. Crypto liquidity sympathy: BTC-DXY 30d correlation likely rising (not explicitly retrieved but DXY +0.04%, BTC -0.63% suggests positive correlation re-emerging) as Japan carry unwind chatter builds and safe-haven USD bid returns. Risk-on rotation stalling: BTC open interest direction flat to down (institutional longs not adding), and the failure to reclaim $80k despite S&P near highs signals crypto is decoupling downward, front-running a broader de-risking. For positioning: if BOJ hikes or MOF intervenes, expect BTC to test $70k-$72k support as yen carry unwind accelerates and levered longs liquidate. Conversely, if FOMC dovish today and oil retreats, BTC could reclaim $80k-$82k as risk-on resumes. Current setup: neutral to bearish; watch USDJPY 160 and FOMC closely—crypto is a high-beta sympathy trade to carry unwind, not a driver.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: 1-2 week risk elevated by three factors: (1) USDJPY 18 pips from 160 with +0.12% daily pace—historically intervention-triggering velocity; (2) BOJ 3-dissent vote (largest hawkish bloc) signals June hike odds rising and inter-meeting move possible if yen collapses; (3) Katayama verbal warnings escalated to 'bold action' language referencing Japan-US joint statement, indicating MOF on high alert. FOMC decision today (April 29) is the near-term wildcard: dovish hold could relieve USD bid and pull USDJPY back to 157-158, downgrading risk; hawkish language or oil >$110 would push through 160, triggering intervention or forced repositioning. Oil at $107/bbl with Hormuz closed sustains imported inflation pressure (¥/bbl 17,090), giving BOJ hawks ammunition. Both weeks grade C (Watchful): meaningful stress building, intervention risk elevated, setup fragile.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: 2-3 week window spans post-FOMC clarity into May, with no major BOJ catalyst until June meeting but rising odds of inter-meeting communication (Ueda speech, board member interviews) signaling policy tilt. If USDJPY breaches 160 in week 2, week 3 becomes the intervention aftermath or forced unwind initiation—historically, MOF interventions (Sept 2022, April/May 2024) provided only 1-2 week relief before resumption of yen weakness, so week 3 could see renewed carry unwind pressure if fundamentals (wide spread, oil >$100) remain intact. Applied unwind prior to week 3 grade C: data ambiguous (spread 187 bps still wide, vol 7.7 still low, no cascade yet) but policy reaction function shifting (BOJ hawks, MOF alert) and oil shock unresolved create a setup where one catalyst flips regime. Without prior, would grade D. Scenario: if FOMC dovish + oil retreats, weeks 2-3 ease to D/D; if 160 breached + pace spikes, weeks 2-3 escalate to B/B (early unwind signatures).
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: 3-4 week spread reflects the divergence between near-term fragility (week 3, grade C with prior applied) and the 4-week resolution window (grade D, Unlikely). Week 4 (late May) sits ~2 weeks before June BOJ meeting, allowing time for either (a) pressure to dissipate if oil retreats, FOMC dovish, or USDJPY pulls back below 158, or (b) intervention to have occurred and market to stabilize at new level (e.g., MOF buys yen, USDJPY drops to 155-157, carry narrows but no full unwind). Grade D at 4 weeks assumes the latter: intervention or policy shift contains the move, and the June BOJ decision becomes the next catalyst rather than an imminent trigger. Non-monotonic grading (C/C/C/D) reflects the setup: weeks 1-3 elevated risk due to proximity to 160, MOF alert, and BOJ pivot, but week 4 downgraded because the intervention/hike catalyst either fires (and stabilizes) or doesn't (and drift continues). If week 3 sees Stage 3 trigger (intervention or hike), week 4 could be Stage 4 (Forced Unwind) or Stage 5 (Capitulation), but base case assumes containment.

watch_list: USDJPY pace through 160: 18 pips away; daily pace >+0.2% or break above 160.50 historically triggers MOF intervention or accelerates BOJ normalization rhetoric.

FOMC decision today (April 29): dovish hold or hint at cuts would relieve USD bid, pull USDJPY back to 157-158, downgrade 1-2w risk to D; hawkish or 'higher for longer' pushes through 160, escalates to B.
Oil trajectory: WTI at $107; if Hormuz reopens or ceasefire progresses, retreat to $90-$95 eases Japan imported inflation, reduces BOJ hawk urgency; sustain >$110 or spike to $120 forces BOJ hand.
BOJ Ueda/board member communication: any inter-meeting speech or interview signaling June hike certainty or raising neutral rate estimates would compress carry expectations, front-running unwind.
JGB 10Y above 2.50%: currently 2.47%; break above April 13 high (2.496%) signals full normalization priced, compressing carry further and raising cross-asset stress risk.
